questions.statement:
<p>Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom.</p>

<p>Play for time</p>
options: To do something in the stipulated time /
To make excuses or do things to gain time /
To play something for the sake of old times /
To play music in the time provided /

questions.statement:
<p><strong>Select the word segment that substitutes (replaces) the bracketed word segment correctly and completes the sentence meaningfully. Select the option &#39;no correction required&#39; if the sentence is correct as given.</strong></p>

<p>The suspect was (taken unawares, without the chance to dispose) the evidence.</p>
options: taken unawares, without the chance to dispose off /
taken unawares, without the chance to dispose of /
taken unaware, without the chance to dispose /

questions.statement:
<p>Choose the alternative which can replace the underlined word without changing the</p>

<p><span style="color: rgb(33, 37, 41);   ">​</span><span style="color: rgb(33, 37, 41);   ">He could rise to the stature because of his <u><strong>invincible</strong></u> courage.</span></p>

<p><span style="font-weight: 700;"><u>Direction</u>: Select the segment of the sentence that contains the grammatical error. If there is no error mark &#39;No error&#39; as your answer.</span></p>

<p>He was one of the (A)/ more unfortunate people (B)/&nbsp; in the world. (C)/ No error. (D)</p>

<p>Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom.</p>

<p>Walk on air</p>

<p>Select the most appropriate option that rectifies the underlined group of words in the given sentence.</p>

<p data-path-to-node="40">The storm having passed and <u><b data-index-in-node="28" data-path-to-node="40">the sun beginning to set, we decided</b></u>&nbsp;to continue our trek toward the summit despite our overwhelming exhaustion.</p>
options: the sun began to set, we decided /
with the sun beginning to set, it was decided /
the sun began setting; we decided /
the sun having begun to set, we decided /
questions.explanation:
The correct answer is '4' i.e. the sun having begun to set, we decided .

Key Points

  • The sentence is structured as an absolute phrase, which means it consists of a noun and a participle that provide additional information about the main clause.
  • The original phrase "The storm having passed and the sun beginning to set" is correct, but the underlined part needs to maintain grammatical alignment.
  • Option 4, "the sun having begun to set," correctly applies the past participle "begun" to match the grammatical construction of the sentence.
  • The other options either disrupt the structure or fail to maintain the correct tense and form.

Correct sentence: The storm having passed and the sun having begun to set, we decided to continue our trek toward the summit despite our overwhelming exhaustion.

Additional Information

  • Absolute Phrases:
    • An absolute phrase modifies the entire sentence, not just a single word.
    • It consists of a noun and a participle (e.g., "the sun having begun").
    • It adds descriptive detail or context to the main clause.
  • Verb Forms:
    • "Begun" is the past participle of "begin" and is correctly used with "having" to indicate a completed action.
    • "Beginning" is the present participle but doesn't fit in this context as it doesn't indicate a completed action.

questions.statement:
<p>Select the most appropriate option to substitute the underlined segment. If there is no need to substitute it, select No improvement</p>

<p>She told the children <u>not to stop</u> the work.</p>
questions.explanation:
<p><span style="font-family: Helvetica, sans-serif; font-weight: 700;">The correct solution is option 4.</span></p>

<p>The given sentence has no grammatical errors. So, no improvement is needed.</p>

questions.statement: <p><strong>In the following question, four words are given out of which one word is incorrectly spelt. Select the incorrectly spelt word.</strong></p>

<p><strong>Select the correct passive voice of the given sentence.</strong></p>

<p>The Archaeological Survey of India is building another museum to house excavated objects from across the country.</p>
options: Another museum is being built by the Archaeological Survey of India to house excavated objects from across the country. /
Another museum is built by the Archaeological Survey of India to house excavated objects from across the country. /
Another museum will be built by the Archaeological Survey of India to house excavated objects from across the country. /
Another museum has been built by the Archaeological Survey of India to house excavated objects from across the country. /

questions.statement:
<p><strong>Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom.</strong></p>

<p><strong>A sentence/a part of the sentence is underlined. Four alternatives are given to the underlined part which will improve the meaning of the sentence. Choose the correct alternative and click the button corresponding to it. In case no improvement is needed, click the option corresponding to &#39;No improvement&#39;.</strong></p>
